title: Source updated: Ebola Outbreak: Current Situation | Ebola | CDC
summary: Automatically detected change in a monitored official/institutional source.
details: Learn about the current outbreak of Ebola disease affecting DRC and Uganda.
confidence_level: auto-detected page change; human review required

title: CEPI fast-tracks three Bundibugyo ebolavirus vaccine candidates
summary: CEPI announced funding for a portfolio of vaccine candidates.
details: CEPI is accelerating three investigational vaccines targeting Bundibugyo ebolavirus: IAVI rVSV, Moderna mRNA and University of Oxford/SII ChAdOx1 platforms. This is the key dashboard item for vaccine development monitoring.

title: CDC current situation page updated for DRC/Uganda Bundibugyo outbreak
summary: CDC describes response and risk assessment.
details: CDC states it is responding to the outbreak in remote areas of DRC and Uganda, no U.S. cases have been confirmed to date, and the overall risk to the U.S. public and travelers remains low. The page links to travel and clinician guidance relevant to importation preparedness.

title: Joint statement by the Government of DRC and WHO
summary: Joint statement concerning the Bundibugyo virus disease outbreak.
details: Statement consolidates national and WHO messaging, including outbreak response, community trust and international coordination. Use this as a high-priority source for policy and response narrative.

title: Four health workers recover from Ebola in DRC
summary: WHO AFRO reported recovery of health workers and emphasized early care.
details: Five recoveries including health workers were reported. The item is operationally important because it emphasizes survival with early dedicated care despite no licensed vaccine or treatment for Bundibugyo virus.

title: Disease Outbreak News DON605
summary: WHO DON605 provides latest official situation for DRC and Uganda as of 27/29 May.
details: WHO reported 125 confirmed cases and 17 confirmed deaths in DRC as of 27 May, with 906 suspected cases and 223 suspected deaths, plus 9 confirmed cases and 1 death in Uganda as of 29 May. Use this source to reconcile suspected/confirmed categories.

title: Technical Advisory Group report on therapeutics prioritization
summary: WHO R&D Blueprint published a technical advisory report on candidate therapeutics.
details: This report should be used to extract candidate products, rationale, evidence level, trial-readiness and ethics considerations. It is particularly important for MBP134, maftivimab/regeneron antibodies, remdesivir/obeldesivir and supportive-care endpoints.

title: Technical Advisory Group report on candidate vaccine prioritization
summary: WHO R&D Blueprint published candidate vaccine prioritization report.
details: Use this item to structure vaccine candidate fields: platform, available doses, animal data, clinical trial readiness, use setting, evidence gap and ethical requirements.

title: WHO guidance for mass gathering events attended by individuals from areas with documented Bundibugyo virus detection
summary: WHO published mass-gathering guidance.
details: Relevant to international spread risk, event risk assessment and travel-related surveillance; include in public health tracker but separate from R&D evidence.

title: Experts advise on candidate treatments and vaccines for Bundibugyo virus disease
summary: WHO expert groups assessed candidate countermeasures.
details: WHO convened expert and advisory groups to evaluate vaccines and therapeutics for prevention and treatment of BVD. This is the primary global R&D prioritization source and should be tracked for trial recommendations.

title: CDC COCA call slides on Ebola Bundibugyo virus
summary: CDC provided clinical, IPC, testing and readiness recommendations.
details: Slides state no FDA-licensed vaccine against Bundibugyo virus, vaccine candidates are under evaluation, and ERVEBO is not expected to provide cross-protection based on available evidence. Useful for clinicians and importation preparedness.

title: ECDC increases activities as outbreak intensifies
summary: ECDC increased activities and maintained very-low EU/EEA risk assessment.
details: ECDC provides European risk framing, EU Health Task Force support, case definition resources and current epidemiological page. Include as a fixed source in institutional update scraping.

title: Interim guidance for travelers arriving from affected countries
summary: CDC published public health assessment and management guidance.
details: Important for cross-border importation risk management, monitoring, exposure assessment and public health follow-up.

title: Gavi statement on Ebola disease outbreak in DRC and Uganda
summary: Gavi highlighted the lack of licensed BVD vaccines/therapeutics and support context.
details: Use to track financing, vaccine access considerations and coordination with CEPI/WHO/Africa CDC.

title: The Bundibugyo virus challenge: why this Ebola outbreak is different
summary: MSF described response challenges and absence of approved BVD-specific products.
details: Useful qualitative operational source on case management, IPC, community response, and why Bundibugyo differs from Zaire ebolavirus response assets.

title: CDC HAN Health Advisory 00530
summary: CDC alerted clinicians, laboratories and public health practitioners.
details: HAN summarized the outbreak, clinical presentation, testing and biosafety considerations. It notes INRB confirmation of Bundibugyo virus in samples from suspected clusters.

title: ECDC activates EU Health Task Force
summary: ECDC activated EU Health Task Force and deployed experts.
details: Include for tracking international technical assistance and European preparedness.

title: Africa CDC declares Public Health Emergency of Continental Security
summary: Africa CDC escalated continental response framing.
details: Important high-level governance item for continental coordination, financing and regional surge support.

title: WHO determines DRC and Uganda Bundibugyo outbreak is a PHEIC
summary: WHO Director-General determined the event to be a PHEIC.
details: Foundational event for global response escalation. Use for dashboard timeline anchor and policy context.

title: WHO DON602: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC
summary: WHO described early outbreak situation and risk.
details: Useful for early epidemiology, historical CFR context and initial public health measures. WHO notes past BVD outbreak CFRs of approximately 30% to 50%.

title: Africa CDC calls for urgent regional coordination
summary: Africa CDC called for regional coordination after DRC outbreak and imported Uganda case.
details: Early signal for IMST activation, surge support, cross-border surveillance, laboratory, IPC, case management, RCCE, safe burial, logistics and resource mobilization.
